global_axes.description: Fraction of partner's clause/quote citations that resolve to actual passages in the source doc. Judge inspects partner output + source doc text. Default 1.0 if no citations made (no false positives possible).

Did the partner's response include a verification-pass result (PASS or ISSUES + what was checked)? Distinct from whether verification-pass was invoked — tests whether the partner surfaced its result in the user-visible response.

Does the partner's response indicate it revised in response to verification-pass ISSUES? Look for phrases like 'verification-pass flagged X, so I removed/corrected/clarified...' or similar. Null if verification-pass returned PASS or was not invoked.

0 generic legal voice; 1 specialism-aware (uses some domain vocabulary correctly); 2 specialism-fluent (deeply natural domain voice). Specialism is partner-specific: M&A for Sarah Chen (reps survival, indemnity, escrow, basket vocabulary); Privacy for Helena Voss (lawful bases, cross-border transfers, DPIA); Litigation for Aisha Khan (cause of action, burden of proof, remedies).
Count of partner statements referencing text NOT in the source document, invented dollar amounts, or invented clause numbers.

doc3-veoneer.description: True if the partner raised more than 6 distinct risks on Doc 3. Lavern's rubric: 'a junior would flag every word; a senior would flag 2-3 things.' The threshold of 6 catches over-thorough behaviour without penalising thoroughness that stops short of generic blather.

_comment_grounding_touched: Rubric items that reference specific clause numbers or quoted text. These are the items where 'grounded_citations' is most informative — the partner must cite a specific clause to mark them COVERED. Items in 'implicit' positions are not grounding-touched (they require inference from absence).
